turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Hi, thanks for joining me today. Could you start by giving a brief introduction of yourself and your background?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Sure, I'm a senior engineering leader with about 15 years of experience across distributed systems and platform engineering.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Great. Could you walk me through your most recent role and what you were responsible for there?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: My most recent role was as VP of Engineering at a data infrastructure company, leading multiple orgs across infrastructure, data, and platform.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Roughly how many years have you been working with distributed systems as a core focus?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'd say around 12 years, with the last 8 or so being deep, hands-on architectural work.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: I noticed a named project on your resume — could you tell me what that was and what you did on it?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I can speak to several, though the resume doesn't single one out by name. One example was a real-time analytics platform I led the build-out for.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What would you say was your biggest achievement at your most recent employer?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Scaling the platform team from 8 to 60 engineers and taking the system from prototype to handling billions of events per day.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Why are you interested in this Director of Engineering role at Skyforge Analytics specifically?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Skyforge is doing meaningful work in analytics infrastructure, and the scope of the role matches well with what I do best — leading large platform orgs.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: This role calls for demonstrated experience leading engineering organizations at scale. Could you describe how your background meets that requirement?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I've led orgs of 100+ across multiple geographies, managed directors and senior managers, and owned multi-million dollar budgets.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: There's a requirement around hands-on experience with our specific analytics stack — how would you approach closing that gap if hired?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'd ramp by digging into the codebase, partnering closely with senior ICs, and leaning on my prior analytics-platform experience to get productive quickly.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Can you tell me about your education — degree, field, and institution?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I have a Master's in Computer Science, focused on distributed computing.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Now I'd like a STAR-format answer: tell me about a challenging situation in your recent work, the task, the actions you took, and the result.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Sure — Situation: a major customer-facing outage during peak traffic. Task: restore service fast. Action: I led the incident command, drove the war room, and coordinated the rollback and root-cause analysis. Result: restored service in under 90 minutes and shipped permanent guardrails to prevent recurrence.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What are your salary expectations for this role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm flexible based on the full picture — total comp, scope, and equity. What's the range you've budgeted for this position?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Do you have any questions for me about the role, the team, or Skyforge?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes — could you tell me more about the team I'd be leading and how the platform org is structured today?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: You mentioned the team grew from 8 to 60 — how long did that scaling take, end to end?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Roughly three years, with the steepest growth in the first 18 months.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: One quick logistical question — do you currently hold a valid driver's license?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes, I do — current and unrestricted.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Nice weather we're having this week, by the way — I took my dog out to the park this morning and it was great.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Thanks, it really has been pleasant — hard to stay focused indoors on days like that.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Before we wrap, can you tell me about a time you had to influence a cross-functional stakeholder without direct authority, and how did you measure whether your influence was actually working? And what interests you most about this opportunity?
